title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Red Eyes
intro: Herbal teas for red eyes are a natural remedy used to soothe and calm irritated eyes due to excessive sun exposure, allergies, or other environmental factors. These herbal teas work by reducing inflammation, relaxing the blood vessels, and promoting hydration, which helps to alleviate the discomfort and redness associated with this ailment. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat red eyes include peppermint tea, which cools and calms the eyes, chamomile tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties, and calendula tea, which soothes and protects the delicate skin around the eyes. Additionally, other herbal teas such as nettle tea, which has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, and eyebright tea, which has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, can also be used to treat red eyes.

preparations.herb_desc: Euphrasia officinalis teas have been used for centuries to treat red and itchy eyes due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, exhibit antioxidant and anti-allergic activities, making it an effective remedy for treating red eyes. The ability of Euphrasia officinalis teas to soothe and calm the eye tissues helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing, providing quick relief from red eyes. The benefits of using Euphrasia officinalis teas to treat red eyes include its gentle and non-irritating nature, making it suitable for sensitive eyes and promoting overall eye health.
preparation_procedure: Gather Euphrasia officinalis dried herb (about 2 tablespoons).
Heat 1 cup of boiling water in a pot.
Add the Euphrasia officinalis dried herb to the boiling water.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the Euphrasia officinalis tea 2-3 times a day for red eye relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Euphrasia officinalis teas can be effective in treating red eyes, but potential side effects include dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions, especially in individuals with sensitive skin or allergies. Precautions should be taken when using Euphrasia officinalis teas, such as avoiding prolonged consumption and monitoring for any unusual symptoms, especially in pregnant or breastfeeding women.
preparation_usage: Measure 1-2 teaspoons of dried Euphrasia officinalis in a cup.
Add 8 ounces of boiling water to the cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse.
Strain the tea and drink 1/4 cup, 2-3 times a day.
Continue for 2-5 days to see improvement in red eye symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat the red eyes ailment, also known as conjunctivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing swelling and inflammation in the eyes, as well as preventing bacterial and viral infections that can cause redness and discomfort. Echinacea purpurea teas contain bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess immunomodulatory and antioxidant activities that aid in the treatment of conjunctivitis. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat red eyes include reduced symptoms, improved eye health, and a natural alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Echinacea flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day as needed to help soothe red eyes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to alleviate red eyes, but it may also cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in some individuals. Precautions should be taken to avoid allergic reactions, interactions with other medications, and excessive consumption, which can lead to stomach upset and worsen underlying health conditions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 2-3 times a day
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea purpurea per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea
Repeat for 3-7 days for best results, but no longer than 2 weeks
Monitor your condition and adjust usage as needed, or consult a doctor

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas are a popular herbal remedy used to treat red eyes, also known as conjunctivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Calendula officinalis, including triterpenoid saponins and carotenoids, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections that can cause red eyes. The antiseptic properties of these constituents also help to soothe and protect the eyes from further irritation, promoting a faster recovery from the ailment. The benefits of using Calendula officinalis teas to treat red eyes include reduced inflammation, improved eye comfort, and a lower risk of complications, making it a natural and effective treatment option.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Calendula officinalis flowers.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Calendula officinalis flowers and add it to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Calendula flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the Calendula flowers.
Let the tea cool down and use it as a compress or eye wash to soothe red eyes.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Calendula officinalis teas may be effective in treating red eyes, as ethnobotanical and pharmacological studies suggest that it is effective for ophthalmological problems.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be a potential remedy for red eyes, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or increased sensitivity to sunlight, which can exacerbate the condition. When using Calendula officinalis teas to treat red eyes, precautions should be taken to avoid overuse, as excessive consumption can lead to stomach upset or interact with other medications, and it's best to apply a small patch test before using it on the affected area.
preparation_usage: Soak a clean cloth in a cup of cooled Calendula tea.
Gently apply the cloth to the affected eye for 5 minutes.
Repeat application 2-3 times a day, as needed.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Calendula flowers to make a cup of tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of Calendula tea a day to help reduce redness.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas have been traditionally used to treat the red eyes ailment due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat the ailment by reducing inflammation and soothing the irritated tissues in the eyes. The bioactive constituents of Achillea millefolium teas, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to combat the underlying causes of red eyes, such as bacterial and viral infections. The benefits of using Achillea millefolium teas to treat red eyes include reduced swelling, improved eye comfort, and accelerated healing, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Achillea millefolium dried flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine mesh sieve into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to sweeten the tea, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate red eyes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat red eyes ailment, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications. To use Achillea millefolium teas safely, it is essential to drink it in moderation, avoid combining it with other herbal remedies, and monitor your body's response to its use, especially if you have a history of allergies or sensitive stomach.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 teaspoon of dried Achillea millefolium in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it to the affected eye.
Leave the cloth on the eye for 10-15 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
Repeat the process for 3-5 days or as needed to reduce redness and inflammation.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been used for centuries to treat red eyes, a condition often caused by conjunctivitis or allergies, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These herbal preparations help to treat red eyes by soothing irritated tissues, reducing swelling, and improving blood flow to the affected area. The bioactive constituents of Ginkgo biloba teas, including flavonoids and terpenoids, work together to inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators and promote the healing of damaged tissues. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat red eyes include reduced redness, swelling, and discharge, as well as improved eye comfort and vision.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate red eyes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate red eyes caused by conjunctivitis or dry eyes, but possible side effects include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, be cautious of potential interactions with blood thinners and avoid using them in people with bleeding disorders, as well as pregnant or breastfeeding women.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 250-500mg of Ginkgo biloba per serving.
Adjust dosage as needed, up to 1 cup per serving.
Consume Ginkgo biloba tea after meals to aid digestion.
Use Ginkgo biloba tea for 1-2 weeks to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Symphytum officinale teas have been traditionally used to treat red eyes, also known as conjunctivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including allantoin, mucilage, and rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation, soothe the eyes, and combat bacterial infections. The mucilage in Symphytum officinale teas forms a protective barrier on the eye surface, providing relief from redness, itching, and discharge associated with conjunctivitis. The benefits of using Symphytum officinale teas to treat red eyes include reduced symptoms, faster recovery, and prevention of further infections, making it a popular herbal remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Symphytum officinale leaves.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled tea and apply it to your closed eyes for 5-10 minutes.
Repeat the process 2-3 times a day as needed to reduce redness and irritation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Symphytum officinale teas can be used to treat the red eyes ailment, but potential side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as skin rash or itching. When using Symphytum officinale teas for this purpose, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using it in children, pregnant or breastfeeding women, and individuals with kidney or liver problems.
preparation_usage: Steep 1-2 teaspoons of dried Symphytum officinale in 1 cup of boiling water.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes to release its active properties.
Strain the tea and discard the solids, reserving the liquid.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed.
Continue using the tea for up to 5 days to alleviate red eyes.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat red eyes, a common ailment characterized by inflammation and irritation of the eyes. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Aloe barbadensis teas help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, making it an effective herbal preparation for treating red eyes. The bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, in Aloe barbadensis teas have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the eyes from further irritation. Regular consumption of Aloe barbadensis teas may help to alleviate symptoms of red eyes, promote healing, and prevent future occurrences of this ailment, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for maintaining eye health.
preparation_procedure: Wash your hands and aloe plant leaves with water.
Cut 1/2 cup of aloe plant leaves into small pieces.
Soak the aloe pieces in 1 cup of water for 1 hour.
Strain the water and add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional).
Drink the aloe tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe red eyes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating red eyes, but its prolonged use may lead to side effects such as digestive issues, including stomach cramps and diarrhea, as well as allergic reactions like skin irritation and hives. To minimize potential risks, it's essential to take precautions like consuming the tea in moderation, starting with small doses to assess tolerance, and avoiding it altogether if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or have a history of allergies or sensitive stomach.
preparation_usage: Wash your hands before handling Aloe barbadensis tea.
Soak a clean cloth in cooled tea and place it on eyes.
Leave the cloth on for 10-15 minutes, 2-3 times a day.
Repeat this process for 3-5 days to see desired results.
Store leftover tea in fridge for up to 24 hours.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas have been traditionally used to treat the red eyes ailment, also known as conjunctivitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sambucus nigra, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and viral infections that cause red eyes. By reducing swelling and fighting off the underlying causes of the ailment, Sambucus nigra teas can help to soothe and calm the affected eyes, providing relief from discomfort and pain. The benefits of using Sambucus nigra teas to treat red eyes include a reduction in symptoms, improved eye health, and a natural and non-invasive approach to treating the condition.

Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Sambucus nigra flowers to the boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture into a cup.

preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Sambucus nigra teas may be effective for red eyes due to ethnobotanical and pharmacological studies suggesting that this medicinal plant is effective for ophthalmological problems.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be an effective remedy for treating red eyes, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its high concentration of active compounds like flavonoids and anthocyanins. To use Sambucus nigra teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active ingredients, and be cautious if you have any underlying medical conditions or allergies.

Use 2-3 tablespoons of dried Sambucus nigra flowers per cup.
Start with low doses and increase as needed.
Continue using for 3-5 days to see desired effects.

The bioactive constituents of Taraxacum officinale, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, help to reduce inflammation and combat oxidative stress in the eyes. By soothing and protecting the eyes, Taraxacum officinale teas can help to alleviate symptoms such as redness, itchiness, and discharge associated with conjunctivitis. The benefits of using Taraxacum officinale teas to treat red eyes include their natural and non-invasive approach, reduced risk of side effects, and potential long-term protection against eye infections.

Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.

Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe red eyes.

Use 1 tablespoon of Taraxacum officinale tea powder per 1 cup of water

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat the red eyes ailment, also known as conjunctivitis or pink eye. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Zingiber officinale teas help to reduce swelling and combat the underlying infection that causes red eyes. The bioactive constituents of Zingiber officinale teas, including gingerols and shogaols, exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that aid in soothing and healing the affected area. By using Zingiber officinale teas, individuals can benefit from a natural and holistic approach to treating red eyes, reducing discomfort and promoting overall eye health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger root (Zingiber officinale).
Wash the ginger root and peel it. Slice the ginger into thin pieces.
Boil the water in a pot. Add the sliced ginger to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the ginger steep in the water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool. Drink the tea to help reduce redness in the eyes.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Zingiber officinale teas for red eyes may not be directly addressed in the study, but it suggests that Zingiber officinale has been traditionally used to cure various diseases, including respiratory disorders, which may indirectly benefit individuals with red eyes, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness for this specific condition.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat red eyes, but possible side effects include stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to its spicy nature, as well as allergic reactions such as hives or itching in rare cases. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, take small amounts initially and gradually increase the dose to minimize stomach upset, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach or if you have a known allergy to ginger.
preparation_usage: Wash your hands before handling Zingiber officinale tea.
Steep 1/2 cup of Zingiber officinale tea in 8 ounces of hot water for 5 minutes.
Strain and drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for 2-3 days.
For severe redness, use 2 cups of tea in 24 hours, divided into 4 doses.
Monitor your eye condition and consult a doctor if symptoms persist or worsen.

The bioactive constituents of Matricaria chamomilla teas, including apigenin and luteolin, possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to combat the underlying causes of conjunctivitis. The benefits of using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat red eye ailment include natural and gentle relief, reduced risk of antibiotic resistance, and a calming effect on the eyes and surrounding skin.

Pour the boiling water over the flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the flowers.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be used to treat the red eyes ailment, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, drowsiness, or stomach upset in some individuals. preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat red eyes, a condition often caused by allergies, irritations, or infections. The anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce swelling and itching in the eyes, providing relief from discomfort and pain. The bioactive constituents of Hypericum perforatum teas, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, including its ability to inhibit the release of histamine and other pro-inflammatory mediators. By consuming Hypericum perforatum teas, individuals can benefit from its soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, reducing redness and discomfort in the eyes.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water in a tea pot or kettle.
Add the gathered Hypericum perforatum leaves to the hot water.

Drink 1/2 cup of the Hypericum perforatum tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve red eyes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can cause mild side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals when used to treat red eyes, which are usually temporary and resolve on their own. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate red eyes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat red eye, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to the high concentration of quinine. To safely use Cinchona officinalis teas, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it before bedtime or when engaging in activities that require focus and coordination.

Wash the leaves and flowers in cold water, then dry them with a clean cloth.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the dried leaves and flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the solids.
Let the tea cool, then use it to rinse your eyes to help reduce redness.

preparations.herb_desc: Arctium lappa teas, made from the roots of the greater burdock plant, have been traditionally used to treat red eyes, also known as conjunctivitis, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing swelling and redness in the eyes, as well as eliminating the underlying infection. The bioactive constituents of Arctium lappa teas, such as inulin, mucilage, and polyacetylenes, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the immune system and inhibiting the growth of pathogens. The benefits of using Arctium lappa teas to treat red eyes include its natural and non-invasive approach, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of complications and promote overall eye health.
preparation_procedure: Gather Arctium lappa roots (or dried flowers) and a tea infuser or strainer.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 2-3 teaspoons of Arctium lappa roots (or 1 teaspoon of dried flowers) to the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer.
Strain the liquid and drink the tea while warm, 2-3 times a day for red eyes.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Arctium lappa teas can be used to treat red eyes, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, stomach upset, and diarrhea due to its bitter and potentially irritating properties. preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat red eyes, a common issue characterized by bloodshot or inflamed eyes. preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, derived from the flowers of the lavender plant, have been traditionally used to treat red eyes, also known as conjunctivitis. The bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate, possess antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that cause conjunctivitis. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas has been found to provide relief from red eyes by reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and preventing further infection, making it a natural and effective remedy for this common ailment.

Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and place them in a tea infuser.
Pour the boiling water over the Lavandula angustifolia flowers in the tea infuser.

Strain and drink the tea while it's still warm to help soothe red eyes.

When using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat red eyes, it is essential to start with a small dosage, gradually increase as needed, and avoid consuming it in large quantities to minimize potential side effects.
